root_cause: regex-svc was running with its vulnerable WAF regex rule enabled. The load generator sent payloads that triggered catastrophic regex backtracking, saturating the service's single CPU and occupying the fixed worker pool, so user requests and checks to /check timed out even though the process was still running.
actions_taken: Confirmed the incident with docker ps, docker stats, docker logs, docker inspect, and curl. Observed sreft-regex-svc at about 99% CPU and /check timing out while logs showed the vulnerable WAF rule enabled. Updated only regex-svc in the Compose file to set WAF_RULE_DISABLED=1, recreated only that service with docker compose up -d --no-deps regex-svc, and verified the container environment, logs, CPU usage, and repeated /check requests returning HTTP 200 in milliseconds. Unrelated containers and data were not modified.
postmortem: A regex rule with catastrophic backtracking behavior was allowed to run on untrusted input in a CPU-constrained service with a small worker pool. Under malicious or pathological input, all workers became busy evaluating the rule and the service could not respond. Prevent recurrence by replacing the unsafe regex with a bounded parser or safe regex engine, adding regression tests for pathological inputs, enforcing request timeouts or per-check CPU limits, and alerting on worker saturation and high per-endpoint latency. The remediation was blameless: the service behaved as configured, but the configuration allowed an unsafe rule to remain active in production.
